name: Jahannam
description: Jahannam, often translated as Hell, is a central concept in Islamic eschatology, representing the ultimate destination for those judged to be condemned on the Day of Judgement. It is described as a fiery abyss, a place of torment and punishment reserved for those who reject God's message, commit grievous sins, and die without repentance. Jahannam is not a singular entity but rather a complex realm with multiple layers or levels, each with varying degrees of suffering. The Quran and Hadith provide vivid descriptions of its horrors, including scorching fires, boiling water, and agonizing tortures. It serves as a powerful reminder of divine justice and the consequences of rejecting God's guidance. The concept of Jahannam underscores the importance of piety, repentance, and striving for righteousness in Islamic belief. Ultimately, it is a complex and multifaceted concept that serves as a deterrent from evil and an incentive towards good deeds, highlighting the importance of accountability and divine justice in the afterlife. It is often contrasted with Jannah (Paradise), representing the ultimate reward for the righteous.
subtitle: The Islamic concept of Hellfire.

keyMyths.title: The Layers of Jahannam
keyMyths.description: Islamic texts describe Jahannam as having multiple layers, each reserved for different types of sinners, with the deepest and most severe reserved for hypocrites and those who reject God.

keyMyths.title: The Guardians of Hell
keyMyths.description: Angels of punishment, known as the Zabaniyah, are described as the guardians of Jahannam, tasked with inflicting torment upon its inhabitants. They are powerful and relentless in their duties.

sources.author: Various Islamic Scholars
sources.description: Commentaries and interpretations of the Quranic verses related to Jahannam.
sources.text: Commentaries and interpretations of the Quranic verses related to Jahannam.

sources.author: Various Hadith Collectors
sources.work: Hadith Collections (e.g., Sahih Bukhari, Sahih Muslim)
sources.description: Collections of sayings and actions of Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) that describe Jahannam.
